response: Sarah owns a small retail shop that supports local factory workers by selling their handmade products. She decides to run a promotional week where she offers a 15% discount on all items produced by the factory workers. If a customer purchases a handcrafted table originally priced at $200 and a set of factory-made chairs originally priced at $150, how much does the customer pay for these items during the promotional week? Remember to apply the discount to each item separately. *** Reach for the discount: calculate the discounted price for each item. The handcrafted table is $200, so 15% of $200 is $30, making the discounted price $170. The factory-made chairs are $150, so 15% of $150 is $22.50, making the discounted price $127.50. The total amount the customer pays is $170 + $127.50 = $297.50. *** The promotional week offers a 15% discount on all items produced by the factory workers. The handcrafted table is $200, so 15% of $200 is $30, making the discounted price $170. The factory-made chairs are $150, so 15% of $150 is $22.50, making the discounted price $127.50. The total amount the customer pays is $170 + $127.50 = $297.50. *** The promotional week offers a 15% discount on all items produced by the factory workers.
